artifacts.summary: Firecrawl search was attempted first for all requested queries but failed because the configured account had insufficient credits. Fallback discovery and direct HTTP fetches found official KU Leuven English guidance for general GenAI use, student assessment and integrity, teaching-staff guidance, safe tool use, and research transparency. Event, faculty-specific, blog, and project pages were rejected and do not support approved claims.

artifacts.claimText: KU Leuven permits and encourages responsible, critical use of generative AI in teaching and research, framing it as a complement to critical thinking and professional expertise rather than a replacement.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI at KU Leuven
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: KU Leuven is open to the use of generative AI (GenAI) in teaching and research, and encourages students, lecturers, researchers and staff to use this technology in a responsible and critical manner. Generative AI is a powerful tool. We use it consciously and purposefully: as a complement to, not a replacement for, critical thinking and professional expertise.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Responsible use of Generative AI in our education
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Teaching staff are expected to clearly inform students about whether or not they are allowed to use GenAI for assignments such as visual, writing and programming assignments. Students are expected to be transparent about the use of GenAI so that their knowledge, understanding and skills can be assessed fairly and correctly.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI at KU Leuven
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Copilot is the preferred tool at KU Leuven. KU Leuven has a contractual agreement with Microsoft that also covers data protection. In addition, our Enterprise environment provides the best technical security, Microsoft does not use entered data for further training, and ICTS ensures the correct settings for all logged-in users.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI at KU Leuven
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: For (strictly) confidential data: use only Copilot logged in with your KU Leuven account. For other data: choose consciously, not automatically. For non-confidential data, you may use other AI tools. For confidential or strictly confidential data, a security check of the tool is mandatory.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidelines for safe use of GenAI tools
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Online, you will find an awful lot of AI tools and more appear every day. Some of those tools are completely unreliable. Therefore, be extra careful when using unsupported tools and when sharing your data. Never fill in personal data, confidential (company) information like IP sensitive data and/or copyrighted material

artifacts.title: KU Leuven staged AI policy crawl
artifacts.summary: Staged crawl for KU Leuven (QS 2026 rank 60; Belgium) found official English-language KU Leuven guidance for responsible GenAI use, student assessment and academic integrity, teaching-staff transparency, Copilot and confidentiality safeguards, safe tool use, and research transparency. Firecrawl search was attempted first but unavailable due insufficient credits; direct official HTTP fetches were used for verified sources. All claims are staged candidates only and require human review before canonical publication.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
This staged run does not publish canonical claims and does not mark any item human_reviewed.
No Dutch or French fallback source was needed because official English sources directly supported the staged claims.
Firecrawl search was unavailable due insufficient credits; discovery used web-search fallback and direct official HTTP fetches.
